name: Cline
description: Open-source autonomous coding agent extension for VS Code

developer.comment: Originally built by Saoud Rizwan; now developed by Cline Bot Inc.

first-release.comment: First released June 2024 under the name Claude Dev; renamed to Cline with v2.0 on Oct 9, 2024 once multi-provider support was added.

status.comment: Actively developed and widely deployed as of 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Agent itself is Apache 2.0 open source (free to use, BYOK); a paid Team/Enterprise tier layers on governance, hence also freemium.

interface.comment: Primarily a VS Code/JetBrains extension; also ships a CLI (npm i -g cline) and an embeddable SDK. Treated as extension + CLI.

host-ides.comment: VS Code (primary), JetBrains (early access), and terminal via the CLI. Also installs in VS Code forks (Cursor, Windsurf).

open-source.comment: Apache 2.0 licensed; agent source is open (the models it drives are not).

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Fundamentally an IDE extension; also distributed as a CLI tool and SDK.

default-model-provider.comment: No single bundled default model; user supplies a provider/key. Anthropic Claude is the most commonly used, but Cline is provider-agnostic.

model-choice.comment: Supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Bedrock, Azure, Vertex, DeepSeek, xAI, Mistral, Cerebras, and any OpenAI-compatible API.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK is the default model — install free, plug in your own API key, pay the provider directly.

local-model-support.comment: Supports local models via Ollama and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

multi-file-editing.comment: Coordinated multi-file changes with linter-aware fixes, diffs, and per-step undo.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Act mode chains edit -> run -> react-to-output autonomously; auto-approve enables full autopilot.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands and reacts to live output (dev servers, tests, deploys).

mcp-support.comment: Native Model Context Protocol server support; can also generate MCP servers.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports multi-agent teams (coordinator delegating to specialists) and a Kanban board for parallel agent runs.

codebase-indexing.comment: Deliberate design choice: no RAG, embeddings, or vector index. Uses agentic file reading / AST traversal at runtime instead — not semantic indexing.

planning-mode.comment: Explicit Plan mode to align on strategy before switching to Act mode to execute; also a /deep-planning command.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input (mockups, bug screenshots) when used with a multimodal model.

browser-computer-use.comment: Built-in browser session: clicks, types, scrolls, captures screenshots and console logs (leverages Claude Computer Use) for interactive debugging and e2e checks.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational edits, step-approval supervised runs, full auto-approve autonomy, and headless async runs in CI (GitHub Actions, GitLab) / via Slack/Linear. No tab-style inline completion.

inline-completion.comment: Agentic chat/edit tool; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Can run headlessly inside GitHub Actions/GitLab pipelines and be driven from Slack/Discord/Telegram/Linear; cron scheduling for recurring automation.

persistent-memory.comment: Project context via .clinerules and a Memory Bank pattern persist guidance across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Built-in checkpoints with one-click undo to restore the working tree to a prior step.

git-integration.comment: Operates on git via terminal commands and dedicated commands/workflows (e.g. /explain-changes for git diffs, PR review/commit flows); no separate native staging UI beyond running git itself.

pr-creation.comment: Can create/review PRs via workflows that call the gh/git CLI and through GitHub Actions integration.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Project-level .clinerules files; also auto-detects AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, and .windsurfrules.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom Workflows/slash commands, Skills (SKILL.md), and an embeddable SDK.

permission-model.comment: Approves every step by default; auto-approve can be toggled per action type for autopilot — i.e. configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Enterprise offering runs on the customer's own infrastructure/inference; self-hosted deployments are supported (telemetry disabled in self-hosted environments).

privacy-mode.comment: Explicit guarantee: Cline never uploads, indexes, or trains on your codebase; no code or prompts used to train/fine-tune models.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise tier offers SSO, RBAC, and OpenTelemetry log export (Datadog/Grafana/Splunk) for audit/governance. VPC not explicitly listed (self-host covers data sovereignty).

pricing-model.comment: Extension is free + BYOK (pay your LLM provider directly). Team/Enterprise adds a per-seat subscription for governance. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: The open-source extension is fully free to use with your own API key; LLM usage cost is paid to the provider.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Core coding use is free (BYOK). The paid Team tier is $20/user/month beyond 10 users, but the free open-source extension covers core use, so entry price is 0.

maturity.comment: Stable, polished, widely deployed (8M+ installs, 62k+ GitHub stars) but still rapidly evolving; not a slow-moving GA enterprise product, so 4 rather than 5.

community-size.comment: Per RESEARCH.md thresholds medium = Cline scale; large and active (8M+ installs, 62k+ stars) but below Copilot/Cursor 'huge'.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Meaningful third-party ecosystem: MCP server marketplace, community .clinerules/workflows, and downstream forks (RooCode, Kilo).

project-memory-files.comment: Native format is .clinerules (file or directory of .md/.txt) -> 'custom'. Cline also auto-detects AGENTS.md (agents-md), .cursorrules (cursorrules), and .windsurfrules (no matching tag). Verified 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Workspace rules in .clinerules/ at project root (project) and global rules in the system Cline Rules directory (user). No documented enterprise/org-managed rules scope. Per-folder scoping is achieved via path-based conditional rules rather than per-directory rule files (see Scoped Rules); GitHub issue #4642 notes folder-scope placement is not yet fully supported.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Default .clinerules are always-on; conditional rules use YAML frontmatter 'paths' glob patterns to activate only for matching files (path-based). No file-type or agent-requested activation documented.

auto-memory.comment: The Memory Bank is a user-triggered pattern — Cline updates it only when the user instructs 'update memory bank' (or via /newrule). It does not autonomously write cross-session memory on its own, so unsupported per the guideline (manual instruction updates do not count).

skill-support.comment: Dedicated Skills feature (SKILL.md directories, added in v3.48) plus reusable Workflows invoked as slash commands — packaged on-demand capabilities beyond plain instruction files.

skill-invocation.comment: Cline auto-activates a skill via the use_skill tool when the request matches the skill's description (model-callable); the user can also force activation with a slash command (user-callable).

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only skill metadata (name + description, ~100 tokens each) stays in context; the full SKILL.md body (<5k tokens) and bundled resources load on demand when the skill is invoked.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills live in ~/.cline/skills (global) or .cline/skills (project); created manually or via the Skills menu and shared with teams by committing to version control (git). No official Cline skills marketplace in the docs, though third-party catalogs (agensi.io/skills, SkillsMP) index community skills. Verified 2026-06-02.
